title.es: NHST Aplicado: OLS, Base Rates e Indicadores Economicos
title.en: Applied NHST: OLS, Base Rates and Economic Indicators

summary_es: Este modulo aplica las lecciones sobre peligros de NHST a tres casos practicos. Primero, se construye un modelo OLS de mercado (Apple vs S&P 500) con Statsmodels, se interpretan p-values, R-squared e intervalos de confianza, y se ejecutan tests diagnosticos que demuestran que los p-values son invalidos con residuales fat-tailed. Segundo, se simulan datos de recesiones tipo NBER para calcular base rates y se construye la confusion matrix de un indicador economico (tipo yield curve), demostrando que una sensibilidad de 85% se traduce en un PPV de solo 43% con base rate de 15%. Tercero, se genera un grafico de PPV vs FPR para diferentes base rates, mostrando que el base rate domina la utilidad real del indicador. Se conecta con la falacia del fiscal aplicada a backtesting financiero.
summary_en: This module applies NHST danger lessons to three practical cases. First, an OLS market model (Apple vs S&P 500) is built with Statsmodels, interpreting p-values, R-squared, and confidence intervals, with diagnostic tests showing p-values are invalid with fat-tailed residuals. Second, NBER-like recession data is simulated to calculate base rates and build a confusion matrix for an economic indicator (yield curve type), showing 85% sensitivity translates to only 43% PPV with 15% base rate. Third, PPV vs FPR curves for different base rates demonstrate that the base rate dominates indicator utility. Connected to the prosecutor's fallacy in financial backtesting.
learning_objectives_es: Construir un modelo OLS con Statsmodels e interpretar summary y diagnosticos
Calcular base rates historicos de recesiones y aplicar Bayes a indicadores
Construir y analizar la confusion matrix de un indicador economico
Cuantificar como el base rate domina el valor predictivo positivo (PPV)
Distinguir P(senal|recesion) de P(recesion|senal) en la practica
learning_objectives_en: Build an OLS model with Statsmodels and interpret summary and diagnostics
Calculate historical recession base rates and apply Bayes to indicators
Build and analyze the confusion matrix of an economic indicator
Quantify how base rate dominates positive predictive value (PPV)
Distinguish P(signal|recession) from P(recession|signal) in practice

parameters.name: Applied Inverse Fallacy
parameters.origin_phrase: 85% sensitivity translates to only 43% PPV with 15% base rate

parameters.name: OLS Assumption Violations
parameters.origin_phrase: Fat-tailed residuals invalidate OLS p-values and confidence intervals

parameters.name: Base Rate Integration
parameters.origin_phrase: Bayes naturally incorporates base rates that NHST ignores
